reasoning: Based on the search results, Donald Trump and Elon Musk experienced a severe falling out in the summer of 2025 over policy disputes, NASA nominations, and Musk launching the "America Party." However, by late October 2025, reports indicated they had resumed communicating and that Musk was "back in Trump's good graces." With the knowledge cutoff at November 9, 2025, there are only about 7 weeks left in the year. While their relationship is highly volatile, these reconciliation cycles typically take months to play out. Since they have recently patched things up, it is more likely than not that this renewed relationship status ("good terms") will hold through the final month and a half of 2025, although the underlying frictions keep the chance of another sudden rupture decently high. I estimate a 73% probability that they remain on good terms on December 31, 2025.
